name: Spanish Potato Omelet (Tortilla a La Española)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Olive oil, or a mixture of o
4 each Large potatoes, peeled and cubed
recipeInstructions:
Heat the oil in an 8 or 9-inch skillet. Add potato slices one at a time so they don't stick together. Alternate layers of potato and onion slices and lightly salt the layers. Cook slowly over medium heat (the potatoes will "boil" in the oil rather than fry), lifting and turning the potatoes occasionally, until tender but not brown. The potatoes should remain separated, not forming a cake.
Drain the potatoes in a colander, reserving about 3 tablespoons of oil. Wipe out the skillet, scraping off any stuck particles (if difficult, wash the skillet; it will be used again to set the omelet and must be completely clean).
In a large bowl, beat eggs with a fork until slightly foamy. Salt to taste. Add potatoes to egg mixture, pressing down so they are fully covered by egg. Let sit for 15 minutes.
Heat reserved oil in skillet until it reaches smoking point. It must be very hot; otherwise, eggs will stick. Add potato and egg mixture, spreading rapidly with a pancake turner. Lower heat to medium-high and shake pan often to prevent sticking. When eggs begin to brown under, invert a plate over skillet and flip omelet onto plate. Add about 1 tablespoon more oil to the pan, then slide omelet back in to brown other side.
Lower heat to medium and flip omelet two or three more times (this helps shape while cooking), briefly on each side. It should be juicy within. Transfer to platter and cool before cutting into thin wedges or 1-1½-inch squares for serving.
